issues.title: CSS color variables use hex/rgb format instead of OKLCH
issues.description: All 98 color variable definitions in colors.css use hex (#ff4890, #1a1a1a, etc.) and rgb() format. The Tailwind rule specifies that CSS variables should use OKLCH format (oklch(L C H)) for better perceptual uniformity and easier color manipulation. While this is a shared package file (not directly in apps/), it is the single source of truth imported by all apps via @import "ui/globals".
issues.fix: Convert hex and rgb color values to OKLCH format. Example: --background: #fff → --background: oklch(1 0 0); --foreground: #1a1a1a → --foreground: oklch(0.13 0 0); --brand: #ff4890 → --brand: oklch(0.65 0.22 350);

issues.title: Arbitrary font sizes when design system values may suffice
issues.description: Font sizes text-[1.55rem], sm:text-[1.8rem], and lg:text-[2rem] use arbitrary values. The theme defines clamp-based utilities (text-cta-display, text-hero-display) and standard Tailwind scale values (text-2xl=1.5rem, text-3xl=1.875rem, text-4xl=2.25rem) that could serve this purpose. The leading-[0.94] is a tight custom line-height for a display heading, which is design-intentional.
issues.fix: Consider using text-3xl (1.875rem) with sm:text-4xl (2.25rem) to align with the design system scale, or extract a semantic utility class (e.g., .text-feature-heading) in utilities.css to avoid repeating these specific arbitrary values.

issues.title: Arbitrary letter-spacing values not in design system
issues.description: tracking-[0.18em] appears twice in this file (lines 31 and 42) for display text. Tailwind's built-in tracking scale (tracking-widest = 0.1em) doesn't cover 0.18em. The store app uses tracking-[0.12em] similarly. These are display/uppercase text patterns repeated across the codebase.
issues.fix: Consider adding a custom tracking value to the @theme block in theme.css (e.g., --tracking-display: 0.18em) to make tracking-display a named utility, avoiding scattered arbitrary values.

issues.title: Redundant @source directive duplicates what shared globals.css already declares
issues.description: Four apps (store, admin, payments, studio) include @source "../../../../packages/ui/src/**/*.{ts,tsx}" in their globals.css. However, this exact directive is already present in packages/ui/src/styles/globals.css, which all apps import via @import "ui/globals". Additionally, three apps (auth, landing, playground) also add @source "../**/*.{ts,tsx}" for their own app sources, while store, admin, payments, and studio do not. This creates a minor inconsistency — the four apps without the explicit app @source rely entirely on Tailwind v4's automatic source detection.
issues.fix: Either (a) remove the redundant @source for ui package from all app globals.css files since the shared globals.css already handles it, OR (b) add consistency by ensuring all apps either include or exclude the explicit @source for their own app files. Document the intentional choice in the shared globals.css comments.

patterns.description: All 7 apps correctly import @import "ui/globals" which centralizes theme, colors, base, and utility definitions — clean single-source-of-truth pattern.
Zero raw Tailwind palette colors (text-red-500, bg-blue-600, etc.) found across all 754 source files. Semantic tokens (destructive, success, warning, info) are used exclusively.
No inline style attributes using hard-coded color values found. All color references go through CSS variables via Tailwind semantic tokens.
candy-safelist.txt is used correctly to force-generate dynamic candy palette classes (bg-pink, bg-mint, etc.) that are composed programmatically — proper Tailwind v4 safelist pattern.
